diagnostics.title: Unversioned localStorage key
diagnostics.message: localStorage.setItem("gve-layout-preferences", JSON.stringify(...)) has no version, so changing the data shape later crashes your users' saved sessions. Add one to the key (e.g. "gve-layout-preferences:v1").
diagnostics.help: Put a version in the storage key (e.g. "myKey:v1"). If you change the data shape later, old saved data can be ignored instead of crashing the app.

diagnostics.title: Unversioned localStorage key
diagnostics.message: localStorage.setItem("gve-autosave-draft", JSON.stringify(...)) has no version, so changing the data shape later crashes your users' saved sessions. Add one to the key (e.g. "gve-autosave-draft:v1").
diagnostics.help: Put a version in the storage key (e.g. "myKey:v1"). If you change the data shape later, old saved data can be ignored instead of crashing the app.

diagnostics.title: Custom modal instead of dialog
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can tab out of this `role="dialog"` modal because it has no built-in focus trapping, so use the native `<dialog>`, which gives you focus trapping, `Escape` to close, and the backdrop for free.
diagnostics.help: Replace the wrapper with `<dialog>` and open it with `dialog.showModal()`. For the trigger, prefer `<button commandfor="id" command="show-modal">` (Chrome 135+), or a `useRef` with `dialogRef.current?.showModal()`.

diagnostics.title: Noninteractive element given interactive role
diagnostics.message: Role `button` gives `<article>` interactive semantics even though the element is noninteractive, so screen reader users get the wrong controls.
diagnostics.help: Use a real interactive element instead of adding an interactive role to a static one.

diagnostics.title: Interactive control contains another focusable control
diagnostics.message: This focusable `<button>` is nested inside an interactive ancestor whose descendants lose their own semantics. Move the inner control outside.
diagnostics.help: Move the inner control beside its interactive ancestor so each action has independent semantics and focus behavior.

diagnostics.title: Interactive control contains another focusable control
diagnostics.message: This focusable `<button>` is nested inside an interactive ancestor whose descendants lose their own semantics. Move the inner control outside.
diagnostics.help: Move the inner control beside its interactive ancestor so each action has independent semantics and focus behavior.

diagnostics.title: Interactive control contains another focusable control
diagnostics.message: This focusable `<button>` is nested inside an interactive ancestor whose descendants lose their own semantics. Move the inner control outside.
diagnostics.help: Move the inner control beside its interactive ancestor so each action has independent semantics and focus behavior.

diagnostics.title: Interactive control contains another focusable control
diagnostics.message: This focusable `<button>` is nested inside an interactive ancestor whose descendants lose their own semantics. Move the inner control outside.
diagnostics.help: Move the inner control beside its interactive ancestor so each action has independent semantics and focus behavior.

diagnostics.title: Handler on non-interactive element
diagnostics.message: Keyboard & screen reader users can't trigger this `<main>` because it isn't interactive, so use a button or link or add an interactive role.
diagnostics.help: Put interactions on a button or link, or add an interactive role.

diagnostics.title: Custom modal instead of dialog
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can tab out of this `role="dialog"` modal because it has no built-in focus trapping, so use the native `<dialog>`, which gives you focus trapping, `Escape` to close, and the backdrop for free.
diagnostics.help: Replace the wrapper with `<dialog>` and open it with `dialog.showModal()`. For the trigger, prefer `<button commandfor="id" command="show-modal">` (Chrome 135+), or a `useRef` with `dialogRef.current?.showModal()`.

diagnostics.title: Context value from an unmemoized local literal
diagnostics.message: Every consumer of this context redraws on each render because its `value` is a fresh object/array/function rebuilt each render — memoize it in component scope (extract mapped providers into a child component first), or move it outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Memoize the context value in component scope so consumers do not redraw every render. For mapped providers, extract each item into a child component and memoize there.

diagnostics.title: Custom modal instead of dialog
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can tab out of this `role="dialog"` modal because it has no built-in focus trapping, so use the native `<dialog>`, which gives you focus trapping, `Escape` to close, and the backdrop for free.
diagnostics.help: Replace the wrapper with `<dialog>` and open it with `dialog.showModal()`. For the trigger, prefer `<button commandfor="id" command="show-modal">` (Chrome 135+), or a `useRef` with `dialogRef.current?.showModal()`.

diagnostics.title: Static value rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `paths` inside `Icon` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
diagnostics.message: "@monaco-editor/react" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
diagnostics.help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

diagnostics.title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
diagnostics.message: "monaco-editor" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
diagnostics.help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Custom modal instead of dialog
diagnostics.message: Keyboard users can tab out of this `role="dialog"` modal because it has no built-in focus trapping, so use the native `<dialog>`, which gives you focus trapping, `Escape` to close, and the backdrop for free.
diagnostics.help: Replace the wrapper with `<dialog>` and open it with `dialog.showModal()`. For the trigger, prefer `<button commandfor="id" command="show-modal">` (Chrome 135+), or a `useRef` with `dialogRef.current?.showModal()`.

diagnostics.title: Pure function rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `minimize` inside `WindowTitleBar` uses no local state but is rebuilt on every render, so it wastes work & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the function above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted work.

diagnostics.title: Pure function rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `close` inside `WindowTitleBar` uses no local state but is rebuilt on every render, so it wastes work & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the function above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted work.

diagnostics.title: Unknown DOM property
diagnostics.message: React ignores this prop because it doesn't recognize the name.
diagnostics.help: Use the prop name React expects, like `className`, `htmlFor`, or `tabIndex`, so the attribute is applied correctly.
diagnostics.suppressionHint: oxlint matches plugin rules only by their full name, so `react/no-unknown-property` in your eslint-disable comment does not silence `react-doctor/no-unknown-property` — add `react-doctor/no-unknown-property` to the comment (e.g. `react/no-unknown-property, react-doctor/no-unknown-property`).

diagnostics.title: Unversioned localStorage key
diagnostics.message: localStorage.setItem("gve-snippets", JSON.stringify(...)) has no version, so changing the data shape later crashes your users' saved sessions. Add one to the key (e.g. "gve-snippets:v1").
diagnostics.help: Put a version in the storage key (e.g. "myKey:v1"). If you change the data shape later, old saved data can be ignored instead of crashing the app.

diagnostics.title: Unversioned localStorage key
diagnostics.message: localStorage.setItem("gve-snippets", JSON.stringify(...)) has no version, so changing the data shape later crashes your users' saved sessions. Add one to the key (e.g. "gve-snippets:v1").

diagnostics.title: .map().filter(Boolean) loops twice
diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
diagnostics.help: Use `.flatMap(item => condition ? [value] : [])` to change and drop items in one pass, instead of building a throwaway array in between

diagnostics.title: Array lookup inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
diagnostics.help: Use a `Set` or `Map` when you check for the same items over and over. `Array.includes`/`find` scans the whole list each time
